name: Intel's Itanium: Ready and Desirable for Mainframe-Class Workloads
description: Aberdeen Group white paper examining whether Intel Itanium processors are ready to handle mainframe-class workloads. Based on interviews with migration users and a survey of 98 mainframe users, the study finds that Itanium platforms match or exceed mainframes in performance, scalability, and cost-effectiveness while being equally robust. 40% of mainframe users express openness to Itanium migration, attracted by TCO advantages.

about.name: Intel Itanium Processor
about.description: 64-bit EPIC architecture processor designed for enterprise workloads

about.name: IBM zSeries Mainframe
about.description: Enterprise mainframe platform dominated by IBM z990 at time of study

about.name: Enterprise Platform Migration
about.description: Process of migrating workloads from mainframe to alternative platforms

variableMeasured.name: Mainframe User Satisfaction
variableMeasured.description: Percentage of respondents satisfied with mainframes

variableMeasured.name: Itanium Workload Confidence
variableMeasured.description: Percentage believing Itanium can handle mainframe workloads

variableMeasured.name: Openness to Itanium Migration
variableMeasured.description: Percentage of mainframe users open to Itanium migration

measurementTechnique: Field research interviews with migration users
